Before → After

BEFORE · EXISTING CONDITION
As-Built · 1960s Brick Ranch
Hip roof · Carport · Small windows · Dated brick · Full gut candidate
AFTER · MCM LUXURY TARGET
Renovated · Modern MCM Luxury
Glass garage · Walnut cladding · Floor-to-ceiling windows · $1.9M ARV
BEFORE → AFTER
◆
Design Reference: Target aesthetic for 5960 Greenbrier Rd gut renovation. Same footprint typology — single-story ranch on wooded lot — repositioned as MCM luxury. Glass garage doors · Walnut tongue-and-groove soffit · Black aluminum window frames · Illuminated landscape · Floating concrete approach.
Renovation Scope

Design Specifications

01
Glass + Aluminum Garage
Full-view glass garage doors in matte black aluminum replacing the existing carport. Interior-lit at night. The single move that signals luxury before the buyer reaches the front door.
02
Walnut Soffit + Cladding
Tongue-and-groove walnut or Ipe wood cladding on the garage fascia and entry zone. Warm amber tones against the repainted white brick — the MCM warmth signature.
03
Floor-to-Ceiling Windows
Existing small windows replaced with full-height black aluminum sliding glass doors and picture windows across the living zone. Indoor-outdoor living fully activated.
04
White Painted Brick Facade
Existing brick painted in warm white or limewashed — preserving the original texture while completely transforming the aesthetic. Clean, light, modern. Cost-efficient and high-impact.
05
Floating Concrete Approach
Large-format concrete pavers replacing the existing cracked driveway. Grass-joint grid pattern. Uplighting embedded at grade. The arrival sequence sets the $1.9M tone.
06
Luxury Interior Core
Open plan gut. White oak floors. Plaster walls. Calacatta marble waterfall island. Primary suite with soaking tub. Brass fixtures throughout. Vittoria Casa staging opportunity on exit.
